name: loomsft
description: Canonical SFT input schema for loomsft.py. One JSON object per line (jsonl). OpenAI-style messages with first-class tool-calling (tool_calls / tool_call_id), the convention adopted broadly across HF transformers, vLLM, and TRL -- not invented here.

role.description: One of: system, user, assistant, tool. Exactly the OpenAI chat-completions role set.

content.description: Turn text. null is valid ONLY on an assistant turn that carries tool_calls instead of (or in addition to) text -- see tool_calls below.

tool_calls.description: assistant-role only. Function calls the model makes this turn. Absent/null on all other roles.

id.description: Call id. The tool-role turn answering this call must echo it back in tool_call_id.

type.description: Always 'function' today (OpenAI convention; the field exists so other tool types can be added later without a schema break).

function.description: {'name': string, 'arguments': string}. 'arguments' is a JSON-ENCODED STRING (matches OpenAI/HF exactly), not a nested object -- this is deliberate: the model must learn to emit valid JSON text, and training on a pre-parsed object would hide that.

tool_call_id.description: tool-role only. Must equal the id of the tool_calls entry this turn is the result of. Absent/null on all other roles.

tools.description: Optional, top-level (per-example, not per-turn). Function/tool definitions available in this conversation, OpenAI function-calling schema. Omit entirely for examples with no tool access rather than passing an empty list -- the renderer treats the two differently (see sft.md).

rules: messages[0].role must be 'system' or 'user' (never 'assistant' or 'tool' -- nothing precedes them to respond to).
every tool-role turn's tool_call_id must match the id of a tool_calls entry in a PRECEDING assistant turn.
content may be null on an assistant turn ONLY if tool_calls is present and non-empty on that same turn.
content must be a non-null string on every system/user/tool turn.
